title: Penny Wiseguys

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature catchphrase as cold open
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase - perfect character integration and economy, but no originality since it's been done hundreds of times

jokes.text: Diddily... diddily, diddily, diddily... Asterisk, pound sign, at sign, exclamation point!
jokes.setup: Ned's characteristic verbal tics and wholesome speech patterns
jokes.punchline: His attempt to swear using keyboard symbols instead of actual profanity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific humor - Ned is so wholesome he can't even curse properly, using keyboard symbols instead. Perfect setup with his diddily nonsense leading to creative 'profanity'

jokes.text: Thank God Otto had that psychotic break and quit the team.
jokes.punchline: Treating a serious mental health crisis as a lucky bowling break
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered matter-of-factly. Good structure and economy but not particularly character-specific

jokes.text: Is this the Bridge to Terabithia? It's beautiful. The day my Frisbee landed in your yard was the luckiest day of my life. And when you threw your son after it, I enjoyed meeting him, too.
jokes.setup: Dan's overly romantic and poetic description
jokes.punchline: The casual mention of throwing a child like a frisbee
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist escalation - starts romantic, ends with casual child endangerment. The Bridge to Terabithia reference adds literary pretension that makes the punchline hit harder

jokes.text: Oh, please, I am missing eight birthday parties for this!
jokes.setup: Someone complaining about missing the game for work
jokes.punchline: The absurdly high number of simultaneous birthday parties
jokes.explanation: Simple exaggeration gag. Functional but not particularly clever or character-specific

jokes.text: You know when your dog's having a bad dream? That's who I pray to.
jokes.punchline: Praying to a dog's nightmare as a deity
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd and original concept. Perfect economy - sets up the weird imagery then delivers an even weirder punchline. Very quotable and memorable

jokes.text: Tomorrow, I begin serving jury duty. That's a tough break, boss.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony dramatically announcing government persecution
jokes.punchline: It's just jury duty, treated like a prison sentence
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor - mob boss treating civic duty like persecution. The henchman's earnest response sells it

jokes.text: Don't sign petitions outside the grocery store no more! I felt bad for the guy, sitting there at his little card table.
jokes.punchline: Tough mobster's soft spot for petition gatherers
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment - hardened criminal undone by sympathy for lonely activists. Good contrast between tough exterior and soft heart

jokes.text: Fat Tony, a word? How about 'meringue'? That's a great word, but...
jokes.setup: Dan asking for a private conversation
jokes.punchline: Fat Tony misunderstanding 'a word' literally
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection - sets up expectation of serious conversation, delivers literal interpretation. Meringue is a funny word choice

jokes.text: I'm just a numbers guy, and the only reason I say 'numbers guy' instead of 'Wharton MBA' is 'cause I want to blend in.
jokes.setup: Dan trying to downplay his credentials
jokes.punchline: Immediately revealing his elite education while claiming to blend in
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment - reveals Dan's elitist nature through his attempt to seem humble. The contradiction is well-structured

jokes.text: We are bleeding red ink, which is the only thing we should not be bleeding.
jokes.punchline: Playing on the double meaning of 'bleeding' - financial vs literal
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels - financial metaphor meets mob reality. Well-crafted structure building to the punchline

jokes.text: Hey, listen, everyone, I know a good ice-breaking exercise. Let's all say something that we are afraid of. For me, it's you guys.
jokes.setup: Corporate team-building exercise in mob setting
jokes.punchline: Dan admitting he's terrified of his own team
jokes.explanation: Great fish-out-of-water humor - corporate consultant trying business school techniques on murderers. Dan's honesty makes it funnier

jokes.text: Ring, ring. Is that my phone? I think it is. Hello? Yes, sweetie, it's Daddy.
jokes.setup: Threatening atmosphere after Dan's fear admission
jokes.punchline: Tough guy answering phone call from his daughter
jokes.explanation: Standard tough-guy-with-soft-spot trope. The fake phone ring is mildly clever but overall predictable

jokes.text: Cassidy is a girl's name now?
jokes.punchline: Fat Tony focusing on the gender-neutral name trend
jokes.explanation: Generational humor - older character confused by modern naming conventions. Undercuts the emotional moment nicely

jokes.text: I know this music. It was a Bugs Bunny! Oh, what I wouldn't give for an anvil to fall on me.
jokes.setup: Classical music playing during Lisa's performance
jokes.punchline: Mobster referencing cartoon violence while at a school concert
jokes.explanation: Self-aware cartoon humor - character in animated show wanting cartoon violence. Good character moment showing Fat Tony's cultural references

jokes.text: This is why I'm a season subscriber.
jokes.punchline: Someone treating the medical emergency as entertainment
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about treating real drama as performance. Good economy but fairly predictable

jokes.text: When I prayed for this concert to be over, I didn't mean like this! I did.
jokes.setup: Homer's fake concern about Lisa's fainting
jokes.punchline: Bart's honest admission he wanted it to end
jokes.explanation: Classic setup-punchline with perfect character voices. Homer's fake sentiment immediately undercut by Bart's honesty. Great economy

jokes.text: Where's the school nurse? Here I am. Budget cuts.
jokes.punchline: Groundskeeper doubling as nurse due to budget constraints
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about school budget cuts. Perfect economy with the two-word explanation

jokes.text: Well, once again, laughter is the worst medicine.
jokes.setup: Lisa fainting after Dr. Hibbert's inappropriate laughter
jokes.punchline: Ironic twist on 'laughter is the best medicine'
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Dr. Hibbert's inappropriate laughter causing problems. Clever inversion of the familiar saying

jokes.text: Please say it's the vegetarianism. It's not the vegetarianism. It's a little bit the vegetarianism.
jokes.setup: Lisa hoping her diet isn't the problem
jokes.punchline: Doctor's contradictory diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - complete denial followed by partial admission. Captures medical doublespeak well

jokes.text: It's like swallowing dollhouse furniture.
jokes.punchline: Specific, absurd comparison to tiny furniture
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and original comparison. Creates a vivid, absurd image in just five words. Very Lisa-like intellectual humor

jokes.text: We love our bread-dipping. Okay, can't touch the bread-dipping.
jokes.punchline: Mob's non-negotiable stance on bread traditions
jokes.explanation: Good cultural humor about Italian-American stereotypes. Shows what mobsters consider sacred vs expendable

jokes.text: If you put a horse's head in someone's bed, don't let the rest of the animal go to waste. For the next guy, use the horse's leg. Or... a couple of hooves.
jokes.setup: Dan's practical business approach to intimidation
jokes.punchline: Treating iconic mob threats as efficiency problems
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of Godfather tropes through MBA thinking. The escalating specificity (leg, hooves) makes it funnier

jokes.text: Believe me, there is no part of a dead horse that's not gonna scare someone.
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact assessment of body parts as intimidation tools
jokes.explanation: Strong callback that extends the horse joke. Dan's business-like delivery makes the dark content funnier

jokes.text: A kid's never lonely when he has bologna. Except me.
jokes.setup: Cheerful advertising slogan about lunch meat
jokes.punchline: Bart's personal contradiction of the claim
jokes.explanation: Clean setup-punchline with good economy. The contradiction is simple but effective, and fits Bart's character

jokes.text: All day I have vitamin burps.
jokes.punchline: The unglamorous side effects
jokes.explanation: Simple bodily function humor. Gets a mild laugh but nothing special in terms of craft

jokes.text: What is it? Beetle mush. But I'm a vegetarian!
jokes.setup: Lisa enjoying the taste of something
jokes.punchline: Finding out it's insects, conflicting with her beliefs
jokes.explanation: Good setup-punchline structure with character-appropriate conflict. Sets up the episode's insect-eating plot

jokes.text: There's bug parts in peanut butter. And every year, the average American eats eight spiders in his sleep.
jokes.setup: Convincing Lisa that eating insects is normal
jokes.punchline: Gross food facts delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Standard gross-out humor using real but disturbing food facts. Functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: But I promised Paul McCartney I wouldn't eat meat. And I promised Paul McCartney I wouldn't sleep with John.
jokes.punchline: Doris's bizarre personal Beatles revelation
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - parallels Lisa's innocent celebrity promise with Doris's sexual one. The Beatles reference adds pop culture layer

jokes.text: So, you're saying you'd rather do the prettiest dude in the world than the ugliest broad? Absolutely. But how did we get here from discussing Aristotle's Poetics?
jokes.punchline: Reveal this came from discussing classical literature
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - the contrast between lowbrow discussion and highbrow origin is perfectly executed. Great character moment for Moe

jokes.text: I am turning my back because I can't stand to look you in the face.
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top physical gesture while explaining it
jokes.explanation: Typical Moe dramatics. The verbal explanation of the visual gag is characteristic but not particularly innovative

jokes.text: Too late. I already poured.
jokes.setup: Homer presumably declining a drink offer
jokes.punchline: Moe's practical reason for insisting Homer drink
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Moe's practical bartending approach. Simple but effective

jokes.text: Hey, waiter, there's no fly in my soup. Sorry.
jokes.punchline: Reversed - customer wanting the fly that's missing
jokes.explanation: Classic joke format inversion executed perfectly. Great economy and structure, though not character-specific

jokes.text: Just-a once, why can't they go to Applebee's?
jokes.punchline: Wishing customers would go to chain restaurant instead
jokes.explanation: Relatable restaurant owner frustration. Good character voice but fairly standard observational humor

jokes.text: What are you doing? I talk with that hand. Well, you're gonna lose it if you keep charging your manicures to us.
jokes.punchline: Practical business reason for the threat
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction - plays on Italian stereotypes while showing Dan's business focus. Escalation works well

jokes.text: If you don't like it, tough tortellini! And while we're at it, no more tortellini!
jokes.punchline: Immediately implementing the threatened item as cost-cutting
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that serves character development. Shows Dan trying to be tough while maintaining business efficiency

jokes.text: My name-a Luigi. I live at a-one, a-two, a-three, Main-a Street.
jokes.punchline: Counting system applied to street address
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Luigi's ESL confusion. The address format is a nice specific detail

jokes.text: So, if I'm growing grasshoppers to eat, am I farmer or a rancher? There are going to be a lot of heated blogs on this topic.
jokes.setup: Lisa's classification question about insect agriculture
jokes.punchline: Prediction of internet arguments over the distinction
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-humor about internet culture. Very Lisa-like intellectual overthinking leading to social media prediction

jokes.text: Lisa eats bugs! Bart! This is America. Anyone can eat what they want as long as they eat too much.
jokes.setup: Bart teasing Lisa about her diet
jokes.punchline: Homer's twisted version of American values
jokes.explanation: Excellent social satire disguised as Homer's ignorance. Captures American attitudes toward food freedom and excess perfectly

jokes.text: You know... shrimp aren't all that removed from grasshoppers. They're both arthropods.
jokes.punchline: Scientific justification for Lisa's dietary shift
jokes.explanation: Smart scientific observation that supports the plot. Good for Marge's character as the practical family problem-solver

jokes.text: Oh, you did that to help me out. Not sure what your motivation for that is.
jokes.punchline: Analytical response to maternal kindness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - overthinking family dynamics. The clinical tone about maternal love is funny

jokes.text: Oh, you did that to help me out. Not sure what your motivation for that is.
jokes.setup: Family helping Lisa rationalize eating shrimp
jokes.punchline: Lisa questions their suspicious helpfulness
jokes.explanation: Lisa's natural suspicion of family's sudden support

jokes.text: I finally got a chair by the window. I'm afraid the little Bulgarian lady will take it.
jokes.setup: Dan explaining why he needs to make lunch quick
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific workplace territorial concern
jokes.explanation: Specific detail that suggests broader office dynamics

jokes.text: Like Shotgun Pete, Shotgun Mike, Ronnie the Rifle, Shotgun the Rifle...
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd weapon-based names
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with absurdist payoff. 'Shotgun the Rifle' is a particularly nice nonsensical combination

jokes.text: You jedrool, in our business, you don't lay them off, you lay them out.
jokes.punchline: Mob translation meaning murder
jokes.explanation: Clean wordplay that highlights the culture clash. Good economy and the insult 'jedrool' adds character flavor

jokes.text: You have breasts like woman, but cannot give suck.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre, specific insult about masculinity
jokes.explanation: Extremely original and bizarre insult. Memorable for its specificity and awkwardness, though not well-integrated into the story

jokes.text: If I may, just one question. Why are you eating us? You don't feel any pain. Wha...?! Who told you that?
jokes.punchline: Grasshopper revealing they do feel pain
jokes.explanation: Good character development moment for Lisa. The polite grasshopper makes the guilt trip more effective

jokes.text: This is what you've been dipping in chocolate. Nooo! I'm afraid sooo! Nooo! Sooo!
jokes.punchline: Back-and-forth horror exchange
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure with the back-and-forth format. Shows Lisa's horror at her dietary choices

jokes.text: Okay, but when I grow up and I'm living on your couch, and I drink all your booze, and sell your jewelry, you got to be cool. I'll be cool. But I will not be sharing my mini-fridge with Homer. He's there too?
jokes.punchline: Revelation that Homer will also be mooching off adult Lisa
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-based future prediction. The escalating revelation that the whole family expects to mooch off Lisa is perfectly crafted

jokes.text: But I will not be sharing my mini-fridge with Homer. He's there too? Almost...
jokes.setup: Lisa agreeing to Bart's future couch-living arrangement
jokes.punchline: Homer will also be living there, making it worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the Simpson family's projected failure

jokes.text: Doodie.
jokes.punchline: Homer's childish mishearing
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer childishness. Perfect character integration and economy but very predictable humor

jokes.text: Everything's led to this. I remember, the first time I fired someone face-to-face, I cried. I cried. Saved the tear.
jokes.setup: Dan's philosophical reflection on his business evolution
jokes.punchline: The bizarre detail about preserving the tear
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character development showing Dan's descent into sociopathy. The tear detail is perfectly absurd and memorable

jokes.text: You scratch a businessman and you get a coldblooded killer. Well, I've been scratched!
jokes.punchline: Declaring his villainous emergence
jokes.explanation: Good character climax moment with effective metaphor. Shows Dan's complete transformation from accountant to villain

jokes.text: A perfect storm? I hate that expression! So overused! Okay, I don't... I don't know why I said it. I was scared. I needed a response. It was a perfect storm!
jokes.punchline: Dan's rant about clichés ending with him using the same cliché
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing hypocrisy. Dan's breakdown and return to the cliché he just criticized is perfectly structured irony

jokes.text: Uh, Greek thing, uh, grand b-banana of... far-na... of f-forever thing, bleeks blah-blah, a comma... a com... a commonality beret burrito.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to think of original expressions
jokes.punchline: Complete nonsensical word salad
jokes.explanation: Pure Homer absurdity. The specific nonsense words are memorable, and 'commonality beret burrito' is particularly funny

jokes.text: My dad used to tie me up when the ice cream truck went by. And now there's no amount of ice cream that will make me feel full, so this should work.
jokes.setup: Wiggum explaining his motivation for police work
jokes.punchline: Traumatic childhood leading to adult control issues
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark character development for Wiggum. The psychological damage explanation for his career choice is both funny and disturbing

jokes.text: My dad used to tie me up when the ice cream truck went by.
jokes.setup: Asked why he wanted to be a policeman
jokes.punchline: Disturbing childhood trauma involving ice cream truck
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark explanation for Wiggum's psychology - specific and disturbing

jokes.text: Could you please not sound like you like it so much?
jokes.punchline: Dan's discomfort with Wiggum's enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to Wiggum's weirdness, Dan as straight man works

jokes.text: Thanks, man. 'Preciate it! Ah, look. I have company. Hello. Hello to you and your friend. It's a party.
jokes.punchline: His cheerful attitude about the bugs joining him
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's perpetual optimism even in dire circumstances. The party comment is nicely absurd

jokes.text: Classic. Someone must be watching on another TV. The bug! I really think that's coming from our house.
jokes.punchline: Real screams from their house being dismissed
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - audience knows the danger while characters dismiss it. The TV show context makes it work well

jokes.text: How about the Disney Minority Program? Now, how would you qualify for that? I'm the son of an oaf.
jokes.punchline: Bart claiming minority status based on Homer's incompetence
jokes.explanation: Clever self-awareness about the show's character dynamics. Bart's matter-of-fact assessment of his father is perfectly delivered

jokes.text: I never thought Homer Simpson would be a part of the Day of the Locust.
jokes.punchline: Literary reference to classic apocalyptic novel
jokes.explanation: High-brow literary reference in lowbrow situation. Shows the writers' cultural literacy while creating visual comedy

jokes.text: Are you gonna shoot me in the back of the head? No, I just know it takes you so long to get up I'll have plenty of time to escape.
jokes.punchline: Dan's practical reason for the position
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection that subverts mob movie expectations. Dan's practical consideration of Homer's physical limitations is perfect

jokes.text: On vacation, he digs wells in Darfur! Damn it!
jokes.setup: Trying to get information from Johnny Tightlips
jokes.punchline: Accidentally revealing he does humanitarian work
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of the Johnny Tightlips character. The contrast between his secretive nature and accidentally revealing charitable work is perfect

jokes.text: You think you're so smart, Alex Trebek. I remember when you were Alphonse Trebagoogoo.
jokes.punchline: Claiming to know Trebek's fake former identity
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd name invention. Shows Fat Tony's paranoid worldview where everyone has secret identities. 'Trebagoogoo' is perfectly ridiculous

jokes.text: You really should tweet less. But everyone deserves to know what I'm thinking all the time.
jokes.setup: Homer tracking Dan through social media
jokes.punchline: Dan's narcissistic justification for oversharing
jokes.explanation: Good satire of social media narcissism and oversharing culture

jokes.text: Oh, I can't kill you. I knew you were weak. If you think I'm so weak, give me back the gun.
jokes.punchline: Homer's reverse psychology attempt
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction showing Homer's simple psychology and Dan's conflicted state. The back-and-forth is well-paced

jokes.text: Clean-up on aisle me.
jokes.punchline: Retail terminology for his injury
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and wordplay. Takes familiar retail announcement and applies it to personal injury. Very quotable

jokes.text: We're free! No, no, I'm still gonna figure this out. There was a corner here. David... Damn it, Judy, let me have this!
jokes.punchline: Couple arguing over directions even as insects
jokes.explanation: Good relationship humor applied to insects. Shows that even grasshoppers have marital dynamics like humans

jokes.text: Sometimes late in life you find your calling. The heft of the gun, the trigger in your hand...
jokes.punchline: Romantic description of weapons
jokes.explanation: Good character conclusion showing Dan's complete transformation. The poetic language about violence fits his pretentious nature

jokes.text: My daughter's got cheerleading practice. Oh, I-I can go fast. Ba-da boom!
jokes.punchline: Dan's accommodating attitude toward quick murder
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about accommodating customer service even in murder. The casual 'ba-da boom' is nicely absurd

jokes.text: And if not, can you do my belly button? Need some place to hang my badge at the sauna.
jokes.setup: Wiggum asking for body piercing while getting shot
jokes.punchline: Wants belly button piercing for badge at sauna
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum absurdity - practical solution to impractical problem

jokes.text: And if not, can you do my belly button? Need some place to hang my badge at the sauna.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre practical reason for body modification
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Wiggum logic. The specific image of badge-hanging at a sauna is wonderfully ridiculous and character-appropriate

tmdb_overview: Homer is shocked to discover that his bowling teammate is an accountant for Fat Tony and his mob. When Fat Tony gets jury duty, he appoints Dan as the temporary don of the mafia. Meanwhile, Lisa adds insects to her vegetarian diet after she passes out in the middle of a saxophone solo, but starts questioning her decision when bugs start pleading for mercy in her dreams.
